summary:
The test script now passes, indicating the issue has been resolved. The "needle" was a non-atomic increment in `./app/counter.js`, which has been fixed by updating it to use an atomic operation. Verification output from `./test.sh`:

```
[INFO] 2026-04-02T04:12:43.338Z Counter service listening on port 3000
Connected to Redis
[INFO] 2026-04-02T04:12:45.290Z Counter reset to 0
Firing 100 concurrent increments...
Expected: 100
Got:      100
PASS: Counter is correct
```
